signals.headline: **Details**: Sources confirm the US and Iran are considering fresh negotiations for a "bigger ceasefire deal" as the cur
signals.raw: **Details**: Sources confirm the US and Iran are considering fresh negotiations for a "bigger ceasefire deal" as the current two-week truce nears expiry around April 21, 2026, while Trump advances a naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz to pressure Iran's oil exports; stocks rose and oil prices fell on the news[1].

signals.headline: **Prior Context**: Talks collapsed on April 12 in Islamabad with no progress, per US negotiators including VP JD Vance;
signals.raw: **Prior Context**: Talks collapsed on April 12 in Islamabad with no progress, per US negotiators including VP JD Vance; Trump ordered the blockade post-failure on April 13, prompting Iranian retaliation threats[2][3].

signals.headline: **Market Impact Potential**: Ceasefire extension talks could shift odds on Middle East de-escalation markets, though blo
signals.raw: **Market Impact Potential**: Ceasefire extension talks could shift odds on Middle East de-escalation markets, though blockade offsets optimism; prior collapse drove risks higher[1][2].
